alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method of dense video captioning generates textual commentaries anchored with single timestamps.
alignments.pub_claim_text: We propose a novel task of dense video captioning focusing on the generation of textual commentaries anchored with single timestamps.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same core contribution (textual commentaries anchored to single timestamps) with essentially identical meaning and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A challenging dataset consisting of almost 37k timestamped commentaries across 715.9 hours of soccer broadcast videos is presented to support the task.
alignments.pub_claim_text: We present a challenging dataset consisting of almost 37k timestamped commentaries across 715.9 hours of soccer broadcast videos.
alignments.rationale: The dataset description is reproduced verbatim in the publication, so the claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method could help satisfy the needs of fans who cannot watch the live game by providing a tool to summarize video content with engagement similar to live commentary.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our method could help satisfy the needs of numerous fans who follow their team but cannot necessarily watch the live game.
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the accessibility conclusion but omits the preprint's emphasis on "engagement similar to live commentary," narrowing the scope to accessibility for fans who cannot watch live.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed benchmark and baseline highlight the difficulty of temporally anchoring commentaries while demonstrating the capacity to generate meaningful commentaries.
alignments.rationale: The specific statement about the benchmark/baseline highlighting temporal anchoring difficulty and demonstrating capacity is not reflected in the publication's listed claims; instead, the publication adds a different conclusion about accessibility.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Gene expression can be used to subtype breast cancer with improved prediction of risk of recurrence and treatment responsiveness over routine immunohistochemistry.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Gene expression can be used to subtype breast cancer with improved prediction of risk of recurrence and treatment responsiveness over routine immunohistochemistry.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this high-level conclusion unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Deep learning algorithms can effectively extract morphological patterns in digital histopathology images to predict molecular phenotypes quickly and cost-effectively.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Deep learning algorithms can effectively extract morphological patterns in digital histopathology images to predict molecular phenotypes quickly and cost-effectively.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves this conclusion verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our model is able to predict gene expression and luminal PAM50 subtype on the TMA dataset with prognostic significance for overall survival in univariate analysis.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed hist2RNA approach predicts gene expression and luminal PAM50 subtype with prognostic significance for overall survival.
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the same predictive and prognostic result but omits the TMA dataset and 'univariate analysis' qualifier, generalizing the claim beyond the specific dataset/analysis reported in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model demonstrates independent significance in multivariate analysis incorporating standard clinicopathological variables.
alignments.rationale: The publication's listed claims do not mention independent significance in multivariate analysis, so this specific statistical claim appears to have been removed from the summarized claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: CherryPicker is an automatic pipeline that reconstructs photo-metric point clouds of trees and performs semantic segmentation and extracts their topological structure in the form of a skeleton.
alignments.pub_claim_text: CherryPicker is an automatic pipeline that reconstructs photo-metric point clouds of trees and performs semantic segmentation.
alignments.rationale: The publication describes the same automatic pipeline but the Q0 summary omits explicit mention of skeleton extraction (which is covered elsewhere), so the claim is presented with slightly less detail.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method presented in CherryPicker automatically estimates the scale factor of a monocular reconstruction to overcome scale ambiguity and obtain metrically correct point clouds.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The method presented in CherryPicker automatically estimates the scale factor of a monocular reconstruction to overcome scale ambiguity.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the scale estimation result with the same intent and effect; the wording is equivalent.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed semantic skeletonization algorithm is built upon Laplacian-based contraction.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The semantic skeletonization algorithm proposed in CherryPicker is based on Laplacian-based contraction.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates this algorithmic detail directly and consistently with the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: CherryPicker effectively removes artifacts induced by occlusion and structural size variations by weighting different tree organs semantically.
alignments.pub_claim_text: CherryPicker effectively removes artifacts induced by occlusion and structural size variations by weighting different tree organs semantically.
alignments.rationale: The result about artifact removal via semantic weighting is preserved unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: CherryPicker obtains high-quality topology reconstructions of cherry trees with precise details.
alignments.pub_claim_text: CherryPicker obtains high-quality topology reconstructions of cherry trees with precise details.
alignments.rationale: The publication reiterates the high-quality topology reconstruction claim identically.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The training process of a deep reinforcement learning (DRL) policy on hardware of a series elastic actuator (SEA) pendulum system improves tracking performance.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The deep reinforcement learning (DRL) policy demonstrates improved tracking performance with a mean absolute error reduction of 25 N after 21 hours of training compared to the first 18 minutes.
alignments.rationale: The publication quantifies the improvement (25 N MAE reduction over training time), making the general preprint claim about improved tracking more specific.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The DRL policy shows a $25$ N improvement in mean absolute error when comparing the first 18 minutes of training to the full 21 hours for a 50 N amplitude, 0.1 Hz sinusoid desired force trajectory.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The deep reinforcement learning (DRL) policy demonstrates improved tracking performance with a mean absolute error reduction of 25 N after 21 hours of training compared to the first 18 minutes.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the 25 N MAE reduction claim exactly though it omits the waveform amplitude/frequency details, so the numeric result is unchanged but some experimental detail is narrowed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The DRL policy exhibits better tracking and stability margins compared to a model-free PID controller for a 50 N chirp force trajectory.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The DRL policy exhibits better tracking and stability margins than a model-free PID controller for a 50 N chirp force trajectory.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this comparative result verbatim, so the claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed weakly supervised method for ICH segmentation utilizes a Swin transformer trained on an ICH classification task with categorical labels.
alignments.rationale: The publication's listed claims do not explicitly state the use of a Swin transformer trained on classification labels; that specific methodological detail is not reflected in the summarized publication claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The technique achieved a mean Dice score of 0.44, demonstrating similar ICH segmentation performance as the U-Net and Swin-UNETR models with full supervision.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed weakly supervised method for ICH segmentation achieved a mean Dice score of 0.44.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the mean Dice 0.44 result; wording differs slightly but the numeric result is identical.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method outperformed a similar weakly supervised approach using GradCAM.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed framework outperformed a similar weakly supervised approach using GradCAM.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the comparative result against a GradCAM-based weakly supervised approach unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Binary ICH classification has a more positive impact on self-attention maps compared to full ICH subtyping.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Binary ICH classification has a more positive impact on self-attention maps compared to full ICH subtyping.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this observation about binary classification and self-attention maps directly.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Heterogeneous Swin Transformer with Multi-Receptive Field (HST-MRF) model improves medical image segmentation by fusing patch information under different receptive fields.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Heterogeneous Swin Transformer with Multi-Receptive Field (HST-MRF) model effectively fuses patch information from different receptive fields to address the loss of feature information caused by patch segmentation.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same core contribution about fusing multi-receptive-field patch information to mitigate feature loss; wording is consistent and the claim content is preserved.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Experimental results demonstrate that the HST-MRF method outperforms state-of-the-art models in polyp and skin lesion segmentation tasks.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method outperforms state-of-the-art models in segmentation tasks for polyp, skin lesion, and breast ultrasound.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the original outperforming claim but expands the scope by adding breast ultrasound, so the claim is clarified and broadened rather than removed or contradicted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Ablation experiments verify the effectiveness of each module in the HST-MRF model and the benefits of multi-receptive field segmentation in reducing the loss of structural information.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Ablation experiments and qualitative analysis confirm the effectiveness of each module and the advantages of multi-receptive field segmentation in preserving structural information.
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the same ablation and qualitative findings about module effectiveness and structural preservation; the meaning is preserved.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The heterogeneous Swin Transformer (HST) achieves interaction of multi-receptive field patch information through heterogeneous attention, enhancing progressive learning.
alignments.rationale: This mechanism-level statement about heterogeneous attention and progressive learning appears in the publication but was not explicitly present in the preprint, so it is treated as an added claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The ptycho-laminographic reconstruction of integrated circuits can be accelerated with 16-times fewer angular samples and 4.67-times faster computation using a physics-regularized deep self-supervised learning architecture.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The ptycho-laminographic reconstruction of integrated circuits can be accelerated with 16 times fewer angular samples and 4.67 times faster computation using a physics-regularized deep self-supervised learning architecture.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the exact acceleration claim with equivalent wording and metrics, so the claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The fidelity of the reconstruction using the proposed method is checked against a densely sampled reconstruction that uses full scanning and no learning.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The fidelity of the reconstruction was checked against a densely sampled reconstruction that uses full scanning and no learning.
alignments.rationale: The fidelity-comparison claim is the same, but the publication presents it with stronger certainty, so confidence is amplified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The self-supervised learning kernel improves reconstruction quality even over the densely sampled reconstruction by filling the missing cone.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The self-supervised learning kernel improves reconstruction quality even over the densely sampled reconstruction by filling the missing cone.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same improvement claim but reports it with slightly lower certainty than the preprint, indicating the claim's confidence was tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Classic rule-based algorithms are completely outperformed by deep learning based methods.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not restate this absolute claim comparing classic rule-based algorithms and deep learning, so the preprint's sweeping conclusion appears to have been removed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We propose a multi-resolution model that sustains high-resolution low-level semantic information throughout.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed ECG continual learning (ECG-CL) approach enhances data usage effectiveness and facilitates inter-task knowledge transfer.
alignments.rationale: The preprint's architectural emphasis on multi-resolution high-resolution preservation is reframed in the publication as an ECG continual learning contribution focused on data usage and transfer; this represents a revision and a narrower framing of the contribution.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our approach successfully extracts informative morphological and rhythmic features from ECG segmentation, leading to higher quality classification results.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The method demonstrates a substantial enhancement of classification accuracy by extracting pertinent morphological and rhythmic features from ECG segmentation.
alignments.rationale: Both statements describe the same outcome: extracting morphological and rhythmic features from segmentation yields substantially improved classification accuracy.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The possibility of a comprehensive ECG interpretation algorithm based on single-lead ECGs is confirmed.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This research confirms the potential for developing comprehensive ECG interpretation algorithms based on single-lead ECGs.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same conclusion about the potential for comprehensive single-lead ECG interpretation, preserving the original meaning.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Our experiments provide evidence of the continual learning method's ability to perform incremental learning across domains, classes, and tasks.
alignments.rationale: This explicit claim about continual learning capabilities across domains, classes, and tasks is present in the publication but was not asserted in the preprint, so it is an added claim.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Leveraging advanced diagnosis algorithms aims to increase the accessibility of ECG monitoring and improve healthcare outcomes.
alignments.rationale: The publication includes an added conclusion about broader aims to increase accessibility and improve outcomes, which does not have a counterpart in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There is a large fraction of near-duplicates in the training and test sets of datasets used in License Plate Recognition research.
alignments.pub_claim_text: There is a large fraction of near-duplicates in the training and test sets of datasets used in License Plate Recognition research.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same finding about near-duplicates; the claim is preserved without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There is a substantial decrease in recognition rate when six well-known models are trained and tested under fair splits, without duplicates.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Training and testing six well-known models under fair splits, without duplicates, results in a substantial decrease in recognition rate.
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the same experimental result about decreased recognition rates under duplicate-free splits; the claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The ranking of models changed considerably when trained and tested under duplicate-free splits in one of the datasets.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The ranking of models changed considerably when trained and tested under duplicate-free splits in one of the datasets.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the reported change in model ranking under duplicate-free evaluation, matching the preprint claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The presence of near-duplicates has significantly biased the evaluation and development of deep learning-based models for License Plate Recognition.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The presence of duplicates has significantly biased the evaluation and development of deep learning-based models for License Plate Recognition.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same conclusion about evaluation bias due to duplicates; the meaning and scope are effectively the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed conditional Bernoulli Diffusion model (BerDiff) enhances the capacity of diffusion models for binary segmentation tasks by using Bernoulli noise as the diffusion kernel.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed conditional Bernoulli Diffusion model (BerDiff) enhances the capacity of diffusion models for binary segmentation tasks by using Bernoulli noise as the diffusion kernel.
alignments.rationale: The publication reiterates the core contribution about using Bernoulli noise for binary diffusion; the claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: BerDiff produces a range of diverse segmentation masks by randomly sampling the initial Bernoulli noise and intermediate latent variables multiple times.
alignments.pub_claim_text: BerDiff produces a range of diverse segmentation masks by randomly sampling the initial Bernoulli noise and intermediate latent variables multiple times.
alignments.rationale: The diversity/sampling behavior claim is present unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: BerDiff can efficiently sample sub-sequences from the overall trajectory of the reverse diffusion, speeding up the segmentation process.
alignments.pub_claim_text: BerDiff can efficiently sample sub-sequences from the overall trajectory of the reverse diffusion, thereby speeding up the segmentation process.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the efficiency/speedup claim but reports it with slightly lower certainty, so confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Extensive experimental results demonstrate that BerDiff outperforms other recently published state-of-the-art methods in medical image segmentation.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Extensive experimental results demonstrate that BerDiff outperforms other recently published state-of-the-art methods in medical image segmentation.
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the same empirical outperforming result; the claim content is retained.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The results suggest that diffusion models could serve as a strong backbone for medical image segmentation.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not explicitly include this broader interpretive conclusion about diffusion models as a strong backbone, so the preprint's concluding statement appears omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed neural network can predict ion concentration profiles in nanochannels with high accuracy.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed neural network serves as a much faster surrogate model for molecular dynamics simulation with high accuracy.
alignments.rationale: The publication combines the accuracy claim with the surrogate-model / speed claim, thereby clarifying and broadening the original accuracy-only statement to also emphasize its surrogate role.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The neural network serves as a much faster surrogate model for molecular dynamics simulation.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed neural network serves as a much faster surrogate model for molecular dynamics simulation with high accuracy.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the fast-surrogate claim but adds explicit emphasis on high accuracy, so the claim is clarified by added accuracy detail.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The neural network demonstrates superior prediction accuracy over XGBoost.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The neural network demonstrates superior prediction accuracy compared to XGBoost.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same comparative-accuracy result against XGBoost with no meaningful change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The neural network is flexible in predicting ion concentration profiles with different bin sizes.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The neural network is flexible in predicting ion concentration profiles with different bin sizes.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same claim about flexibility across bin sizes without substantive change.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Modeling the ion concentration profile as a probability distribution enhances the prediction capabilities of the neural network.
alignments.rationale: This methodological contribution (modeling as a probability distribution) does not appear in the preprint and is newly emphasized in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Self-training Semantic Cross-attention Ranking (sRank) framework achieves an 11.7% gain in offline top-one accuracy on the Smart Reply task over the previous system.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Self-training Semantic Cross-attention Ranking (sRank) framework achieves an 11.7% gain in offline top-one accuracy on the Smart Reply task over the previous system.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the exact quantitative result with no change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: sRank enables a 38.7% time reduction in composing messages in the Smart Reply task.
alignments.pub_claim_text: sRank enables a 38.7% time reduction in composing messages in the Smart Reply task.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains this performance/time-reduction claim verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: In the Ambient Clinical Intelligence task, sRank achieves a 35.5% top-one accuracy gain and a 46% relative ROUGE-L gain in generated medical notes.
alignments.pub_claim_text: In the Ambient Clinical Intelligence task, sRank achieves a 35.5% top-one accuracy gain and a 46% relative ROUGE-L gain in generated medical notes.
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the same numerical gains and task results without modification.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The underlying decision problems related to ordinal motifs are NP-complete.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The underlying decision problems related to ordinal motifs are NP-complete.
alignments.rationale: The NP-completeness result is stated identically in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Ordinal motifs can be incrementally identified to save computational effort.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Ordinal motifs can be incrementally identified to save computational effort.
alignments.rationale: The content is the same but the publication presents this as a higher-confidence/result-level claim (certainty increased to high), so confidence is amplified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Ordinal motifs can be leveraged to retrieve basic meaning from a medium sized ordinal data set.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Ordinal motifs can be leveraged to achieve textual explanations based on principles from human computer interaction.
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the outcome from a general "retrieve basic meaning" to a more specific goal of producing textual explanations grounded in HCI principles, which revises and narrows the original claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The paper surveys the latest advancements in cybercrime prediction by reviewing more than 150 research articles.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This paper surveys the latest advancements in cybercrime prediction by reviewing more than 150 research articles.
alignments.rationale: The survey scope and contribution are stated the same in both preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The review highlights the use of machine and deep learning techniques to detect anomalous behavior and identify potential threats.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The review highlights the use of machine and deep learning techniques to detect anomalous behavior and identify potential threats.
alignments.rationale: The methodological emphasis is unchanged between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Transfer learning is discussed as a method to adapt models trained on one dataset for use on another dataset in the context of cybercrime prediction.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Transfer learning is discussed as a method to adapt models trained on one dataset for use on another dataset.
alignments.rationale: The claim about transfer learning is preserved; the publication wording is slightly more general but the meaning is the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The paper identifies critical innovations, research gaps, and future research opportunities in cybercrime prediction.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The paper identifies critical innovations, research gaps, and future research opportunities in cybercrime prediction.
alignments.rationale: The identification of gaps and future directions is consistently reported in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: RAPID can learn stable resource allocation policies in minutes, as compared with hours in prior state-of-the-art.
alignments.pub_claim_text: RAPID can learn stable resource allocation policies in minutes, as compared with hours in prior state-of-the-art.
alignments.rationale: The claim about fast policy learning is unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: RAPID improves Quality of Service (QoS) by 9.0x.
alignments.pub_claim_text: RAPID improves Quality of Service (QoS) by 9.0x.
alignments.rationale: The QoS improvement figure is identical in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: RAPID increases best-effort workload performance by 19-43%.
alignments.pub_claim_text: RAPID increases best-effort workload performance by 19%–43%.
alignments.rationale: The reported performance range is the same (minor formatting difference only).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Effective resource sharing remains an open challenge due to the adverse effects of resource contention on high-priority workloads.
alignments.rationale: The discussion of open challenges/resource contention present in the preprint does not have a direct counterpart in the publication and appears to have been omitted or replaced.

alignments.pub_claim_text: RAPID leverages lightweight QoS predictions to guide policy learning at a rate orders of magnitude faster than prior work.
alignments.rationale: This specific emphasis on leveraging lightweight QoS predictions to accelerate learning is newly stated in the publication and was not present in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The scaled DW-MTJ neuron demonstrates voltage-dependent firing probability.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The scaled DW-MTJ neuron demonstrates voltage-dependent firing probability.
alignments.rationale: The preprint and publication text are effectively identical in wording and meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The simulated SNN using the DW-MTJ neuron attains accuracy during learning comparable to a more complicated multi-weight DW-MTJ device.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The simulated SNN using the DW-MTJ neuron attains accuracy during learning comparable to a more complicated multi-weight DW-MTJ device.
alignments.rationale: The claim is the same in both versions with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The binary DW-MTJ neuron outperformed other devices during inference after gaussian noise was introduced to the Fashion-MNIST classification task.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The binary DW-MTJ neuron outperformed other devices during inference after Gaussian noise was introduced to the Fashion-MNIST classification task.
alignments.rationale: Only minor orthographic capitalization differs; the substantive claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DW-MTJ devices can be used to construct noise-resilient networks suitable for neuromorphic computing on the edge.
alignments.pub_claim_text: DW-MTJ devices can be used to construct noise-resilient networks suitable for neuromorphic computing on the edge.
alignments.rationale: Conclusion is worded identically and retained without modification.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The article provides a systematic analysis of explainable artificial intelligence (XAI) in healthcare.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The article provides a systematic analysis of explainable artificial intelligence (XAI) in healthcare.
alignments.rationale: The contribution claim is the same in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The review identifies prevailing trends in XAI and outlines major research directions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The review identifies prevailing trends in XAI and outlines major research directions.
alignments.rationale: The result statement is preserved verbatim between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The work contributes to the formalization of the XAI field.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The examination of XAI methodologies contributes to the formalization of the XAI field.
alignments.rationale: The publication makes explicit that it is the examination of methodologies that contributes to formalization, a minor clarification of the original claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A comprehensive examination of XAI methodologies is presented, focusing on their implications in healthcare.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The article discusses the implications of using XAI models in healthcare.
alignments.rationale: The published wording tones down the scope (from a "comprehensive examination" focused on implications to a more general "discusses"), indicating a narrowed and slightly tempered presentation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: RescueSNN improves accuracy by up to 80% while maintaining the throughput reduction below 25% in high fault rate scenarios.
alignments.pub_claim_text: RescueSNN improves accuracy by up to 80% while maintaining the throughput reduction below 25% in high fault rate scenarios.
alignments.rationale: The quantitative result is preserved exactly between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Permanent faults in SNN chips can significantly degrade accuracy and cause system malfunctions.
alignments.rationale: The publication's listed claims do not include this general statement about the impact of permanent faults, so the preprint claim appears omitted from the publication summary.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed methodology, RescueSNN, mitigates permanent faults without requiring additional retraining.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed methodology mitigates permanent faults in SNN chips without requiring additional retraining.
alignments.rationale: The contribution claim about mitigation without retraining is retained verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The impact of permanent faults in SNN chips and respective mitigation techniques have not been thoroughly investigated yet.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The impact of permanent faults in SNN chips and respective mitigation techniques have not been thoroughly investigated yet.
alignments.rationale: The limitation statement is present and unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The deep learning-based pipeline achieved 89.42% accuracy for the binary classification task using Long Short Term Memory (LSTM) with BERT Embedding.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The deep learning-based pipeline achieved 89.42% accuracy for the binary classification task using Long Short Term Memory (LSTM) with BERT Embedding.
alignments.rationale: Exact result duplicated in publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The multi-label classifier, a combination of Convolutional Neural Network and Bi-directional Long Short Term Memory (CNN-BiLSTM) with attention mechanism, achieved 78.92% accuracy and 0.86 as weighted F1-score.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The multi-label classifier, a combination of Convolutional Neural Network and Bi-directional Long Short Term Memory (CNN-BiLSTM) with attention mechanism, achieved 78.92% accuracy and 0.86 as weighted F1-score.
alignments.rationale: The multi-label classifier performance metrics are unchanged between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The dataset prepared for this study consists of 16,073 instances, with 8,488 labeled as toxic and corresponding to one or more of six toxicity categories.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A manually labeled dataset consisting of 16,073 instances was prepared, with 8,488 identified as toxic comments.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the dataset size and toxic-count but omits the explicit mention of the six-category correspondence and states the dataset is manually labeled (higher certainty), narrowing the scope of the original description while increasing confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Local Interpretable Model-Agnostic Explanations (LIME) framework was utilized to explain the predictions and interpret the word feature importance during classification.
alignments.rationale: The publication's listed claims do not include the use of LIME for explanation, so this methodological claim appears omitted from the publication summary.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The ability to describe at a high-level of abstraction how code will behave over all possible inputs correlates strongly with code writing skills.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The ability to describe how code behaves over all possible inputs correlates strongly with code writing skills.
alignments.rationale: Minor wording change (removal of "high-level of abstraction") does not alter the substantive correlation claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: LLM-created explanations are rated as being significantly easier to understand and more accurate summaries of code than student-created explanations.
alignments.pub_claim_text: LLM-created explanations are rated as significantly easier to understand than student-created explanations.
alignments.rationale: The publication splits the combined preprint claim; the matched part (easier to understand) is preserved, though the preprint also asserted greater accuracy (handled by a separate publication claim).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Existing pedagogical approaches that scaffold the ability to explain code do not currently scale well to large classrooms.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Existing pedagogical approaches for explaining code do not scale well to large classrooms.
alignments.rationale: The limitation about scalability is preserved in the publication with equivalent meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The recent emergence of powerful large language models may offer a solution for generating explanations to scaffold students' ability to understand and explain code.
alignments.rationale: The publication's claim list does not include this forward-looking contribution about LLMs as a pedagogical solution, so it appears omitted from the published summary.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Existing transport-based sampling methods have limitations when dealing with unnormalized target density functions that contain isolated modes. (certainty=high, type=limitation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Existing transport-based sampling methods have limitations when dealing with unnormalized target density functions and isolated modes. (certainty=high, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same limitation with effectively identical wording and emphasis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method, TemperFlow, adaptively learns a sequence of tempered distributions to progressively approach the target distribution. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: TemperFlow adaptively learns a sequence of tempered distributions to progressively approach the target distribution. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same mechanism description but reports it with lower stated certainty (high -> medium), so the claim content is unchanged while confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: TemperFlow overcomes the limitations of existing transport-based sampling methods. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method, TemperFlow, effectively addresses the multimodality issue in sampling from high-dimensional distributions. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the general 'overcomes limitations' statement into a more specific claim about addressing multimodality in high-dimensional sampling, narrowing the scope of the original conclusion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Experiments demonstrate that TemperFlow has superior performance compared to traditional sampling methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Experiments demonstrate that TemperFlow outperforms traditional sampling methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Experimental superiority is asserted in both preprint and publication with essentially identical wording and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: TemperFlow is applicable in modern deep learning tasks such as image generation. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: TemperFlow has applications in modern deep learning tasks, such as image generation. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Both versions claim applicability to deep learning tasks (image generation) with similar wording and medium certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A photometric correction model for infrared sensors based on temperature constancy improves the estimates of both camera motion and scene structure. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed photometric correction model for infrared sensors improves the estimates of both camera motion and scene structure in 3D reconstruction. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same contribution claim with equivalent scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The integration of the photometric correction model into an SfM framework demonstrates significant improvements in 3D reconstruction from infrared images. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Experimental evaluations demonstrate the effectiveness of the photometric correction model in the context of Structure from Motion. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the preprint's SfM integration result as experimental validation of effectiveness; wording is slightly less strong about 'significant improvements' but the substantive claim about SfM effectiveness remains.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Reconstruction quality from corrected infrared imagery achieves performance on par with state-of-the-art reconstruction using RGB sensors. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The reconstruction quality from corrected infrared imagery achieves performance on par with state-of-the-art reconstruction using RGB sensors. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The claim about parity with RGB-based state-of-the-art is preserved verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Seven selected nanopriming treatments significantly increase the stress resistance index (SRI) by 13.9% and 12.6% under salinity stress and combined heat-drought stress, respectively. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Seven selected nanopriming treatments significantly increase the stress resistance index (SRI) by 13.9% and 12.6% under salinity stress and combined heat-drought stress, respectively. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Numeric results and interpretation are identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ZnO nanopriming treatment, with the highest SRI value, mainly regulates the pathways of amino acid metabolism, secondary metabolite synthesis, carbohydrate metabolism, and translation. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: ZnO nanopriming treatment, with the highest SRI value, mainly regulates the pathways of amino acid metabolism, secondary metabolite synthesis, carbohydrate metabolism, and translation. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The mechanistic pathways attributed to ZnO treatment are carried over unchanged into the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The concentration, size, and zeta potential of nanoparticles are identified as dominant factors for correlating root dry weight under salinity stress. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The concentration, size, and zeta potential of nanoparticles are identified as dominant factors for correlating root dry weight under salinity stress. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The identification of nanoparticle physical properties as dominant factors is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This work offers a promising framework for accelerating the agricultural applications of nanomaterials and may profoundly contribute to nanosafety assessment. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This work offers a promising framework for accelerating the agricultural applications of nanomaterials and may contribute to nanosafety assessment. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication tones down the strength of the nanosafety impact (removing 'profoundly'), so the overall conclusion is clarified and the claimed confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The novel continual learning method based on multifidelity deep neural networks limits catastrophic forgetting. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The novel continual learning method based on multifidelity deep neural networks limits catastrophic forgetting. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The core result about limiting catastrophic forgetting is stated identically in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The multifidelity continual learning method shows robust results that limit forgetting across several datasets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The multifidelity continual learning method shows robust results that limit forgetting across several datasets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The claim of robust, dataset-spanning performance is preserved without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The multifidelity method can be combined with existing continual learning methods to further limit catastrophic forgetting. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The multifidelity method can be combined with existing continual learning methods to further limit catastrophic forgetting. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The composability claim is present and unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed continual learning method is especially suited for physical problems where data satisfy the same physical laws. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed continual learning method is especially suited for physical problems where data satisfy the same physical laws. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The suitability for physics-governed problems is retained; only the label changed from contribution to conclusion, not the substance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed hierarchical co-attention propagation network (HCPN) is capable of tracking and segmenting objects in zero-shot video object segmentation (ZS-VOS). (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed hierarchical co-attention propagation network (HCPN) is capable of tracking and segmenting objects in zero-shot video object segmentation (ZS-VOS). (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The capability claim for HCPN in ZS-VOS is stated the same in both documents.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model utilizes multiple collaborative evolutions of the parallel co-attention module (PCM) and the cross co-attention module (CCM) to enhance performance. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The parallel co-attention module (PCM) captures common foreground regions among adjacent appearance and motion features. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication breaks the preprint's combined-module statement into more specific functional claims about PCM (and separately CCM); the functionality is clarified and reported with stronger certainty, so confidence is amplified.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The cross co-attention module (CCM) further exploits and fuses cross-modal motion features returned by PCM. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication explicitly states a separate, detailed claim about CCM's role that was not separately enumerated as an individual claim in the preprint (preprint bundled PCM and CCM together).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: FlexMoE can achieve superior performance compared with existing systems on real-world workloads. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: FlexMoE can achieve superior performance compared with existing systems on real-world workloads. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same high-certainty result without substantive change in wording or scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: FlexMoE outperforms DeepSpeed by 1.70x on average and up to 2.10x. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: FlexMoE outperforms DeepSpeed by 1.70x on average and up to 2.10x. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The numerical performance claim is identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: FlexMoE outperforms FasterMoE by 1.30x on average and up to 1.45x. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: FlexMoE outperforms FasterMoE by 1.30x on average and up to 1.45x. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The performance numbers and claim are unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Sparsely-gated Mixture-of-Experts (MoEs) may not be effective in practical systems due to routing imbalance and fluctuation problems. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Sparsely-gated Mixture-of-Experts (MoEs) may not be effective in practical systems due to routing imbalance and fluctuation problems. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The substantive caution about MoEs is retained verbatim; only the claim type label changed from 'limitation' to 'conclusion' without altering content or certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed deep learning model can accurately predict energy demand with a mean absolute error of 1.4%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed REDf model can accurately predict energy demand with a mean absolute error of 1.4%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The core quantitative result is identical, but the publication clarifies the model name (REDf), a minor specification that does not change scope or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed model enhances the stability and efficiency of the power grid. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed model has the potential to enhance the stability and efficiency of the power grid. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the same conclusion about grid stability and efficiency, framed with similar certainty (potential).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model has the potential to effectively manage the integration of renewable energy sources. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The integration of renewable energy sources can be improved by providing accurate predictions of energy demand. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Both claims link accurate demand prediction to improved renewable integration; the publication restates this causal connection without altering scope or certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The approach aligns with SDG 13 on climate action by enabling more efficient management of renewable energy resources. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The approach aligns with the United Nations Sustainable Development Goals 7, 9, and 13. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication expands the policy alignment from SDG13 alone to multiple SDGs (7, 9, and 13) and reports higher certainty, thus broadening and strengthening the original contribution claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The artificial intelligence framework developed combines a neural network with automatic differentiation to recover unknown parameters from experimental data. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A data-driven analysis tool leveraging neural implicit representations can efficiently obtain unknown parameters from experimental data. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the contribution in terms of neural implicit representations (a clarification of the method) while preserving the core claim of efficient parameter recovery.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model predicts the unknown parameters with excellent agreement relative to analytical fitting. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Linear spin wave theory simulations were employed to train a machine learning platform for precise exchange parameter extraction. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication provides a more specific experimental context (exchange parameters via linear spin wave simulations) than the broader preprint claim, narrowing the result to a concrete parameter and methodology.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The approach allows for real-time application to multi-dimensional scattering data without the need for human-guided peak finding and fitting algorithms. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The machine learning platform was applied to inelastic neutron scattering data on the square-lattice spin-1 antiferromagnet La2NiO4. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication replaces the broader methodological claim about general real-time multi-dimensional data with a specific application to La2NiO4 scattering data, narrowing scope while presenting stronger (high-certainty) evidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This prototypical approach promises a new technology for automatically detecting and refining more advanced models for ordered quantum systems. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This work showcases a viable pathway towards automatic refinement of advanced models for ordered magnetic systems. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the forward-looking claim with slightly different wording (magnetic systems) but preserves the scope and level of certainty, clarifying the application domain.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed direct microseismic imaging framework based on physics-informed neural networks (PINNs) can generate focused source images even with very sparse recordings. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed direct microseismic imaging framework based on physics-informed neural networks (PINNs) can generate focused source images even with very sparse recordings. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The key result is carried forward verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The modified representation of the frequency-domain wavefield inherently satisfies the boundary conditions, helping to avoid difficulties in balancing data and PDE losses in PINNs. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The modified representation of the frequency-domain wavefield inherently satisfies the boundary conditions, helping to avoid difficulties in balancing data and partial differential equation losses in PINNs. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The contribution is restated with equivalent meaning; only PDE was expanded to 'partial differential equation' in wording.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The causality loss implementation with respect to depth enhances the convergence of PINNs. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The causality loss implementation with respect to depth enhances the convergence of PINNs. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the same contribution claim without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The numerical experiments on the Overthrust model show that the proposed method can admit reliable and accurate source imaging for single- or multiple-sources in passive monitoring settings. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The numerical experiments show that the proposed method can admit reliable and accurate source imaging for single or multiple sources in passive monitoring settings. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The experimental result is consistent between versions; the publication omits the specific 'Overthrust' mention in the short claim but preserves the result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method produces results that are consistent with numerical methods but less noisy compared to the time-reversal method. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method produces results that are consistent with numerical methods but less noisy compared to the time-reversal method. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The claim about relative noisiness and consistency is unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The CL-Drive dataset includes EEG signals, ECG, EDA, and eye tracking data collected from 21 subjects under various driving conditions. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The CL-Drive dataset includes EEG signals, ECG, EDA, and eye tracking data collected from 21 subjects under various driving conditions. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The dataset composition and subject count are identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The dataset provides subjective cognitive load as ground truth, reported by drivers every 10 seconds during the experiment. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The dataset provides subjective cognitive load as ground truth, reported by drivers every 10 seconds during the experiment. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Benchmark classification results for different machine learning and deep learning models were provided for both binary and ternary label distributions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Benchmark classification results for different machine learning and deep learning models were provided for both binary and ternary label distributions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Benchmarking on binary and ternary labels is carried forward unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Models were trained on both hand-crafted features and raw data using 10-fold and leave-one-subject-out evaluation criteria. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Models were trained on both hand-crafted features and raw data, evaluated using 10-fold and leave-one-subject-out (LOSO) criteria. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The evaluation protocols are the same; the publication clarifies the LOSO acronym but does not change the claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Neural operators have shown considerable promise for fast approximating solution operators of partial differential equations (PDEs).
alignments.pub_claim_text: Neural operators have shown considerable promise for fast approximating solution operators of partial differential equations (PDEs).
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same high-level claim verbatim, with no change in scope or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed variational operator learning (VOL) framework reduces dataset costs and enables label-free learning for solving PDEs.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed variational operator learning (VOL) paradigm provides a unified framework for training neural operators and solving PDEs using domain knowledge.
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the contribution as a unified paradigm emphasizing domain knowledge rather than explicitly claiming reduced dataset costs and label-free learning, shifting emphasis and broadening the framing.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: VOL achieves matrix-free approximation of system functional and residual through Ritz and Galerkin approaches with finite element discretization.
alignments.pub_claim_text: VOL achieves matrix-free approximation of the energy functional of physical systems with linear time complexity and O(1) space complexity.
alignments.rationale: The publication specifies the target (energy functional) and adds explicit complexity guarantees (linear time, O(1) space), making the original matrix-free statement more specific and stronger.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: VOL demonstrates a decrease in test errors in a power law with respect to the amount of unlabeled data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: With a label-free training set, VOL learns solution operators with test errors decreasing in a power law with respect to the amount of unlabeled data.
alignments.rationale: Both preprint and publication state the same empirical scaling law for test error versus unlabeled data (power law) with equivalent scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This study is the first to integrate the weak form and efficient iterative methods for solving sparse linear systems into the end-to-end operator learning task.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This study is the first to integrate the weak form and efficient iterative methods for solving sparse linear systems into the end-to-end operator learning task.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same novelty claim without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our proposed active learning framework effectively reduces the required training data for accurate action spotting in football videos.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our proposed active learning framework effectively reduces the required training data for accurate action spotting in football videos.
alignments.rationale: The claim is reproduced identically in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We achieve similar performances for action spotting with NetVLAD++ on SoccerNet-v2, using only one-third of the dataset.
alignments.pub_claim_text: We achieve similar performances for action spotting with NetVLAD++ on SoccerNet-v2, using only one-third of the dataset.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this empirical result without change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our approach validates its effectiveness across different action semantics in football.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our approach validates its effectiveness across different action semantics in football.
alignments.rationale: No substantive change between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our active learning framework would support further applications of action spotting algorithms and accelerate annotation campaigns in the sports domain.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our active learning framework would support further applications of action spotting algorithms and accelerate annotation campaigns in the sports domain.
alignments.rationale: The concluding claim is preserved with equivalent certainty and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study provides a new synthetic RGB-D dataset for training multimodal instance segmentation models.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A new synthetic dataset tailored to various hand detection tasks was introduced.
alignments.rationale: Both mention a synthetic dataset release, but the publication frames it specifically for hand detection (narrower) versus the preprint's broader RGB-D instance segmentation dataset.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The use of multimodal input, including both colour and depth information, improves the accuracy of the model prediction.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed approach employs multimodal inputs that combine color and depth information to enhance robustness in unstructured environments.
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the multimodal-input claim but shifts emphasis from accuracy improvement generally to enhanced robustness in unstructured environments, changing the evaluated outcome and adding contextual specificity.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Models trained solely on the synthetic dataset outperform corresponding models trained on existing state-of-the-art datasets in terms of Average Precision and Probability-based Detection Quality.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The models achieved higher Average Precision and Probability-Based Detection Quality despite the absence of real-world training data.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the empirical performance claim (higher AP and PDQ) for models trained on the synthetic data, matching the preprint statement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed Edge-Cloud Collaborative Knowledge Transfer Framework (ECCT) enables bi-directional knowledge transfer between the edge and cloud.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed Edge-Cloud Collaborative Knowledge Transfer Framework (ECCT) enables bi-directional knowledge transfer between edge and cloud by sharing feature embeddings and prediction logits.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the bi-directional knowledge transfer claim but clarifies the mechanism (sharing embeddings and logits), adding technical detail without changing scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ECCT enhances personalization, enables model heterogeneity, tolerates training asynchronization, and relieves communication burdens.
alignments.pub_claim_text: ECCT enhances personalization, enables model heterogeneity, tolerates training asynchronization, and relieves communication burdens.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this set of claimed benefits verbatim, with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Extensive experiments on public and industrial datasets demonstrate ECCT's effectiveness and potential for use in academia and industry.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Extensive experiments on public and industrial datasets demonstrate the effectiveness of ECCT.
alignments.rationale: The publication removes the explicit claim about potential use in academia and industry, narrowing the original claim to effectiveness demonstrated by experiments.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The combination of stochastic magnetic tunnel junction (sMTJ)-based probabilistic bits (p-bits) with Field Programmable Gate Arrays (FPGA) creates an energy-efficient CMOS + X prototype.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The combination of stochastic magnetic tunnel junction (sMTJ)-based probabilistic bits (p-bits) with Field Programmable Gate Arrays (FPGA) creates an energy-efficient CMOS + X prototype.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this contribution claim without change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: sMTJ-based p-bits can replace up to 10,000 CMOS transistors while dissipating two orders of magnitude less energy.
alignments.pub_claim_text: sMTJ-based p-bits can replace up to 10,000 CMOS transistors while dissipating two orders of magnitude less energy.
alignments.rationale: The empirical/architectural replacement and energy claims are preserved exactly in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The stochasticity of sMTJs can augment low-quality random number generators (RNG).
alignments.pub_claim_text: The stochasticity of sMTJs can augment low-quality random number generators (RNG).
alignments.rationale: The publication retains this claim with similar certainty and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Integrated versions of the proposed approach can advance probabilistic computing involving deep Boltzmann machines and other energy-based learning algorithms.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Integrated versions of the proposed approach can advance probabilistic computing involving deep Boltzmann machines and other energy-based learning algorithms with extremely high throughput and energy efficiency.
alignments.rationale: The publication adds stronger performance descriptors (extremely high throughput and energy efficiency), clarifying and strengthening the forward-looking potential of integrated versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The design of the pretraining and downstream models significantly impacts the effectiveness of pretrained embeddings in e-commerce applications. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The design of the pretraining and downstream models significantly impacts how information is encoded and decoded via embedding vectors. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same core idea but frames it in terms of encoding/decoding via embeddings (mechanism) and drops the e-commerce application restriction, broadening the claim while preserving confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A principled perspective of pre-trained embeddings through kernel analysis can evaluate their predictability in a scalable manner. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A principled perspective of pre-trained embeddings can be established through kernel analysis, which aids in evaluating their predictability. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication presents the same kernel-analysis perspective to evaluate predictability but omits the explicit 'scalable manner' phrasing, slightly rewording/clarifying the claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There is a lack of thorough understanding of how pre-trained embeddings work, particularly their intrinsic properties and interactions with downstream tasks. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not retain this explicit limitation; instead it emphasizes practical guidance and contributions, so the preprint limitation statement was dropped in the final claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed RGB-T tracker based on a mixed attention mechanism achieves complementary fusion of modalities. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: An RGB-T tracker based on a mixed-attention mechanism (MACFT) is proposed to achieve complementary fusion of modalities. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same contribution claim about a mixed-attention RGB-T tracker achieving complementary fusion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The mixed attention operations enable information interaction and self-enhancement between the template and search images. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: An RGB-T tracker based on a mixed-attention mechanism (MACFT) is proposed to achieve complementary fusion of modalities. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication summarizes the technical effect of mixed attention at a higher level (complementary fusion) and does not explicitly mention the template/search interaction detail, so the preprint's specific mechanism is compressed into a broader claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The modality-adaptive fusion network suppresses low-quality modality noise while enhancing the information of the dominant modality. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed tracker effectively suppresses low-quality modality noise while enhancing information from the dominant modality. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: This result is carried forward verbatim in the publication, keeping the same emphasis on suppressing low-quality modalities and enhancing the dominant one.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Evaluation on multiple RGB-T public datasets demonstrates that the proposed tracker outperforms other RGB-T trackers on general evaluation metrics. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Evaluation on multiple RGB-T public datasets demonstrates that the proposed tracker outperforms other RGB-T trackers on general evaluation metrics. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The evaluation claim is preserved exactly in the publication, asserting superior performance on benchmark datasets.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A machine learning protocol can predict the ground state and its properties of quantum many-body systems up to a prescribed prediction error, provided a sample set can be efficiently prepared and measured. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A machine learning protocol can predict the ground state and its properties of quantum many-body systems up to a prescribed prediction error, provided a sample set can be efficiently prepared and measured. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains this core result verbatim regarding the ML protocol for predicting ground states given efficiently prepared/measured samples.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: An exponential scaling for the provable sample complexity, $N=m^{\mathrm{O}(1/\varepsilon)}$, is universal for generic gapped Hamiltonians. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not retain the preprint's strong universal exponential-scaling claim; instead the final paper emphasizes polynomial bounds, so the exponential universality statement was dropped.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A polynomial sample complexity for predicting quantum many-body states and their properties can be achieved by preserving the fundamental properties of density matrices and utilizing the continuity of quantum states. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The sample complexity for predicting quantum many-body states and their properties is polynomial with respect to the prediction error and the number of qubits. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication affirms the polynomial sample complexity claim and states it more directly in terms of dependencies (error and qubits), clarifying the original mechanism-based description.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The number of samples required for learning local quantum-state properties can be further reduced exponentially with respect to the number of qubits. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: If restricted to learning local quantum-state properties, the number of samples required can be further reduced. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the local-property reduction claim but weakens it: the explicit exponential reduction with qubit number from the preprint is not asserted in the final paper, so the claim is revised and tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The results provide theoretical guarantees for efficient learning of quantum many-body states and their properties, applicable beyond ground states of gapped Hamiltonians. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The continuity assumption suggests that the results are not limited to gapped Hamiltonian systems and properties within the same phase. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the conclusion that the results extend beyond gapped Hamiltonians but ties that extension explicitly to a continuity assumption, clarifying the conditions for generality.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Similarity-Aware Multimodal Prompt Learning (SAMPLE) framework incorporates prompt learning into multimodal fake news detection. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Similarity-Aware Multimodal Prompt Learning (SAMPLE) framework was proposed to address challenges in multimodal fake news detection. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication presents the same contribution claim about proposing the SAMPLE framework for multimodal fake news detection.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: SAMPLE reduces memory usage significantly while achieving comparable performances to fine-tuning. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication's main claims focus on performance gains and feasibility, but it does not explicitly repeat the preprint's specific memory-reduction / comparable-to-fine-tuning statement, so that detail was omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: SAMPLE surpasses the F1 and accuracy metrics of previous works on two benchmark multimodal datasets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: SAMPLE outperformed previous work, achieving higher F1 and accuracy scores on two multimodal benchmark datasets. (certainty=high, type=result)
